content.full_text:
September 7, 1976

A David Stutz, Attorney
Mitchell, Schmidt, D'Amico, McCabe & Stutz
2170 Fourth Avenue
San Diego, California 92101

Dear Mr. Stutz:

This letter is to confirm our telephone conversation of August
17, 1976, concerning a state legislator who is a candidate for
reelection.

You stated that the legislator plans to have an advertising
agency, in which his administrative assistant has a financial
interest, place newspaper advertising relative his reelection campaign. You asked whether the Political Reform Act prohibits this arrangement. I responded that the legislator may
retain the services of the agency as long as the costs incurred
are fully disclosed, either by the candidate, his committee or
the advertising agency. Government Code Section 84303 states
that no expenditure may be made by an advertising agency on behalf or for the benefit of a candidate or committee unless the
payments made by the agency are reported by either the candidate,
committee or the agency. The agency is obligated to make known
to the candidate or committee all information required to be
reported under the campaign disclosure provisions of the Act.

If we can be of further assistance, please contact our staff.

Sincerely,

Nan Hambleton
Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

bcc: Jeanne P.

LAW OFFICES

JOHN A. MITCHELL
DONALD W. SCHMIDT
SEBASTIAN D'AMICO
JOHN J. McCABE, JR.
A. DAVID STUTZ

MITCHELL, SCHMIDT, D'AMICO, McCABE & STUTZ
225 BROADWAY
SAN DIEGO, CALIFORNIA 92101

AREA CODE 714
TELEPHONE 236-1133

August 19, 1976


Ms. Nan Hamilton
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Technical Assistant Division
1015 "L" Street
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Hamilton:

On August 17, 1976 you rendered an opinion concerning
the following set of facts:
Encumbant state legislator is running for re-election.
His properly organized and registered Campaign Committee
receives funds on behalf of the campaign with advertising
agency for legitimate campaign purposes. Legislator's
Administrative Assistant has a financial interest in the
advertising agency.
Question posed: Is there anything illegal about the
above set of facts?

Your oral answer to the above question indicated that
there was currently no statutory prohibition or regulation
involving the above transaction, provided it is all properly
reported and for proper campaign purposes.

It would be greatly appreciated if you could state your
opinion in writing for my file. Thank you in advance for
your cooperation.


Sincerely,

A. DAVID STUTZ

ADS/avg

sections.conclusion: Government Code Section 84303 states that no expenditure can be made by an advertising agency on behalf of or for the benefit of a candidate or committee unless the payments made by the agency are reported by either the candidate, committee or the agency. The agency is obligated to disclose all information required to be reported under the campaign disclosure provisions of the Act.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether a state legislator who is a candidate for reelection can have an advertising agency with which his administrative assistant has a financial interest prepare advertising materials relative to his reelection campaign under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Under Government Code Section 84303, advertising agency expenditures on behalf of a candidate must be reported by the candidate, committee, or agency, and the agency must disclose all information required under the campaign disclosure provisions of the Political Reform Act.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is severely damaged/degraded with significant text loss throughout. No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION section headers present. The response letter structure and content clearly indicates this is an FPPC advice letter. Synthetic summaries based on partially legible text; actual question and conclusion text has multiple gaps and illegible portions.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether a state legislator who is a candidate for reelection can have an advertising agency with which his administrative assistant has a financial interest prepare advertising materials relative to his reelection campaign under the Political Reform Act.

CONCLUSION: Government Code Section 84303 states that no expenditure can be made by an advertising agency on behalf of or for the benefit of a candidate or committee unless the payments made by the agency are reported by either the candidate, committee or the agency. The agency is obligated to disclose all information required to be reported under the campaign disclosure provisions of the Act.

embedding.summary: FPPC response confirming telephone conversation regarding whether a state legislator-candidate can use an advertising agency in which his administrative assistant has a financial interest, advising that agency expenditures must be reported and disclosed per Government Code Section 84303.
